seoTitle: Fuji Electric Products | IGBT Module, IPM, SiC MOSFET | BeiLuo
seoDescription: Browse Fuji Electric products including IGBT modules, discrete IGBTs, IPM modules, and SiC MOSFETs. Technical support available for industrial drives, renewable energy, and EV applications.

faqs.question: What product categories does Fuji Electric offer?
faqs.answer: Fuji Electric offers four main product categories: IGBT Modules including 6th and 7th generation modules in various configurations; Discrete IGBTs for power conversion applications; IPM Modules with integrated drive and protection; and SiC MOSFETs for high-efficiency systems. Each category covers voltage ranges from 600V to 3300V to meet diverse application requirements.
faqs.decisionGuide: Browse our product categories below or contact our FAE team for selection guidance.

faqs.question: How do I select the right Fuji Electric product for my application?
faqs.answer: Selecting the right Fuji product involves: (1) Identify your application requirements - voltage rating, current capacity, switching frequency, and package type. (2) Review the product category that matches your needs - IGBT modules for industrial drives, IPM for integrated solutions, or SiC for highest efficiency. (3) Compare specifications using our parametric tables. (4) Check reference designs and application notes. (5) Contact our FAE team for technical consultation.
faqs.decisionGuide: Contact our FAE team for personalized product recommendations based on your specific requirements.

faqs.question: What are the advantages of Fuji's 7th generation IGBT technology?
faqs.answer: Fuji's 7th generation IGBT technology provides several advantages: (1) Lower Vce(sat) - significantly reduced conduction losses compared to 6th generation. (2) Faster switching - reduced switching losses enable higher switching frequencies up to 20kHz. (3) Better SOA - improved safe operating area for reliable short-circuit protection. (4) Lower EMI - optimized switching characteristics reduce electromagnetic interference. (5) Higher reliability - advanced chip design and packaging technology. (6) Pin compatibility - drop-in replacement for 6th generation in many cases.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use 7th generation IGBTs for new designs requiring highest efficiency and reliability.

categories.name: IGBT Modules
categories.description: Fuji IGBT modules include 6th and 7th generation devices in 2-Pack, 6-Pack, and PIM configurations for industrial drives and renewable energy.
categories.longDescription: Fuji Electric's IGBT module portfolio leverages advanced 6th and 7th generation technology to deliver industry-leading performance. The product range includes 2-Pack modules for half-bridge applications, 6-Pack modules for three-phase inverters, and PIM (Power Integrated Module) configurations with integrated rectifier and brake. Voltage ratings from 600V to 1700V and current ratings from 10A to 1400A cover applications from small servo drives to large industrial inverters and renewable energy systems. As an authorized distributor, we provide comprehensive IGBT Modules selection guidance (选型指导) and technical support.

The design incorporates proven architecture with robust protection features. Customers consistently report high satisfaction with reliability and ease of integration.

faqs.question: What is the difference between 6th and 7th generation Fuji IGBT modules?
faqs.answer: Fuji's 7th generation IGBT modules offer significant improvements over 6th generation: (1) Lower Vce(sat) - approximately 15-20% reduction in conduction losses. (2) Faster switching - reduced turn-off time and tail current. (3) Better trade-off between conduction and switching losses. (4) Improved safe operating area (SOA). (5) Higher reliability with advanced chip design. (6) Better thermal performance. The 2MBI200VH-120-50 uses 7th generation technology, making it ideal for new designs requiring highest efficiency.
faqs.decisionGuide: For new designs, always choose 7th generation. For existing 6th generation designs, evaluate upgrade path based on efficiency requirements.

faqs.question: How do I calculate power losses for the 2MBI200VH-120-50?
faqs.answer: Power loss calculation for 2MBI200VH-120-50: (1) Conduction loss = Vce(sat) × Ic × duty cycle. At 150A RMS with 1.7V Vce(sat) and 80% modulation: Pcond = 1.7 × 150 × 0.8 = 204W per IGBT, 408W total. (2) Switching loss = (Eon + Eoff) × fsw. At 8kHz with typical Eon=15mJ, Eoff=25mJ: Psw = 40mJ × 8kHz = 320W total. (3) Total loss = 728W. (4) For thermal design with Rth(j-c)=0.08°C/W and Rth(c-s)=0.15°C/W, heatsink thermal resistance should be <0.25°C/W to keep Tj<150°C at 50°C ambient.
faqs.decisionGuide: Calculate losses at maximum operating conditions; design thermal management for worst-case scenario.

faqs.question: What gate drive voltage is recommended for 2MBI200VH-120-50?
faqs.answer: Recommended gate drive for 2MBI200VH-120-50: (1) Turn-on voltage: +15V ± 0.5V for full conduction. (2) Turn-off voltage: -5V to -8V for reliable turn-off and dv/dt immunity. (3) Gate resistance: 3.3Ω to 10Ω depending on switching speed requirements. Lower resistance for faster switching (higher EMI), higher resistance for slower switching (lower EMI). (4) Gate drive power: approximately 2W per IGBT at 8kHz. (5) Use isolated gate drivers with >2500Vrms isolation. (6) Implement active Miller clamp for high dv/dt applications.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use +15V/-8V gate drive for industrial applications; adjust gate resistance based on EMI requirements.

faqs.question: Can I parallel multiple 2MBI200VH-120-50 modules?
faqs.answer: Yes, 2MBI200VH-120-50 modules can be paralleled for higher current: (1) Static current sharing - modules from same production lot have matched Vce(sat) within ±0.1V. (2) Dynamic current sharing - symmetric layout with equal DC bus inductance is critical. (3) Common gate drive - use single gate drive with sufficient current capability (>10A peak). (4) Individual gate resistors - not needed for modules sharing same gate drive. (5) Layout - parallel at DC terminals, not through long PCB traces. (6) Derating - parallel 3 modules for 2.5x current capability. (7) Testing - verify current sharing with Rogowski coils under full load.
faqs.decisionGuide: Contact our FAE for parallel operation design review and layout recommendations.

faqs.question: What protection features should be implemented?
faqs.answer: Recommended protection for 2MBI200VH-120-50: (1) Overcurrent protection - desaturation detection with <5μs response time. (2) Short-circuit protection - soft turn-off to prevent overvoltage spikes. (3) Overvoltage protection - active clamping or TVS diodes for inductive spikes. (4) Overtemperature protection - monitor NTC thermistor, shutdown at 125°C heatsink. (5) Undervoltage lockout - disable gate drive if Vge < 12V. (6) Shoot-through protection - hardware interlock and dead-time control. (7) Gate protection - TVS diodes to protect against overvoltage (>20V).
faqs.decisionGuide: Implement all protections for reliable operation; desaturation detection is essential for short-circuit protection.

faqs.question: What is the maximum switching frequency for 2MBI200VH-120-50?
faqs.answer: Maximum switching frequency depends on thermal constraints: (1) Recommended operating frequency: 2-15kHz for industrial drives. (2) Maximum practical frequency: 20kHz with adequate cooling. (3) Higher frequencies increase switching losses significantly - at 20kHz, switching losses are 2.5x higher than at 8kHz. (4) For high-frequency operation (>15kHz), consider SiC hybrid modules or full SiC solutions. (5) Thermal simulation recommended for frequencies above 10kHz. The 2MBI200VH-120-50 is optimized for 2-10kHz operation typical of industrial motor drives.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use 2-10kHz for standard industrial drives; consider SiC for frequencies above 15kHz.

faqs.question: What applications is the 6MBI100VB-120-50 best suited for?
faqs.answer: The 6MBI100VB-120-50 is optimized for: (1) Servo drives - 0.75kW to 7.5kW servo motor control with high dynamic response. (2) CNC machines - spindle drives and axis control requiring precise speed and torque control. (3) Pumps and fans - energy-efficient motor control with V/f or vector control. (4) HVAC systems - compressor and fan control for air conditioning. (5) Conveyor systems - continuous duty industrial applications. The 100A rating supports up to 15kW continuous output at 380V AC with proper thermal design. The compact size is ideal for space-constrained installations.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use for servo drives up to 7.5kW or general purpose inverters up to 15kW.

faqs.question: How does the 6-Pack configuration simplify inverter design?
faqs.answer: The 6-Pack configuration provides several design advantages: (1) Reduced component count - six IGBTs and diodes in one package vs. six discrete devices. (2) Matched characteristics - all devices from same wafer lot ensure balanced current sharing. (3) Simplified layout - compact package reduces PCB area and parasitic inductance. (4) Built-in thermistor - integrated temperature sensing eliminates external sensor. (5) Consistent thermal performance - all devices on common ceramic substrate. (6) Reduced assembly time - single component mounting vs. six discrete devices. (7) Improved reliability - fewer interconnects reduce failure points. Overall, 6-Pack modules can reduce inverter assembly time by 50% compared to discrete solutions.
faqs.decisionGuide: Choose 6-Pack for three-phase inverters under 22kW to simplify design and improve reliability.

faqs.question: What is the recommended DC bus voltage for 6MBI100VB-120-50?
faqs.answer: Recommended DC bus voltage for 6MBI100VB-120-50: (1) For 380V AC input (Europe/Asia): DC bus = 540-600V. Well within 1200V rating with >50% margin. (2) For 460V AC input (North America): DC bus = 650-750V. Still provides adequate margin. (3) Maximum recommended DC bus: 900V to ensure sufficient margin for switching spikes. (4) For 220V AC input: DC bus = 310-350V. Consider 600V rated modules for cost optimization. (5) Voltage ripple: keep below 5% of DC bus voltage with adequate DC link capacitance. The 1200V rating provides flexibility for various input voltages while maintaining safety margins.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use for 380V and 460V AC systems; 1200V rating provides excellent safety margin.

faqs.question: What cooling is required for 6MBI100VB-120-50?
faqs.answer: Cooling requirements for 6MBI100VB-120-50: (1) Thermal resistance Rth(j-c) = 0.35°C/W per IGBT. (2) For 50A RMS output (75A peak) at 8kHz: total losses approximately 150W. (3) Heatsink thermal resistance calculation: Rth(s-a) = (Tjmax - Ta)/P - Rth(j-c) - Rth(c-s) = (150-50)/150 - 0.35 - 0.2 = 0.12°C/W. (4) Recommended heatsink: forced air cooling with thermal resistance <0.15°C/W. (5) Thermal interface: silicone grease or phase change material with <0.3K·cm²/W thermal resistance. (6) Mounting torque: 2.5-3.5 N·m per M4 screw. (7) Airflow: minimum 2m/s across heatsink fins.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use forced air cooling for continuous operation; natural convection only for light loads (<30A).

faqs.question: Can 6MBI100VB-120-50 be used for servo drive applications?
faqs.answer: Yes, 6MBI100VB-120-50 is excellent for servo drives: (1) Current rating - 100A continuous supports servo motors up to 7.5kW. (2) Switching speed - fast switching characteristics suitable for high-performance servo control. (3) Compact size - fits in space-constrained servo drive enclosures. (4) Short-circuit capability - 10μs withstand time for reliable protection. (5) Low inductance - minimizes voltage spikes during fast current changes. (6) NTC thermistor - enables accurate over-temperature protection. (7) Field-proven - widely used in industrial servo systems. Compatible with both asynchronous and synchronous servo motors with vector control.
faqs.decisionGuide: Ideal for servo drives 0.75kW to 7.5kW; supports high dynamic performance requirements.

faqs.question: What is the difference between VB and VH series 6-Pack modules?
faqs.answer: VB vs VH series comparison: (1) VB series - standard performance, optimized for cost-sensitive applications, Vce(sat) ~1.8V. (2) VH series - high performance, optimized for low losses, Vce(sat) ~1.65V. (3) Switching speed - VH series has faster switching with lower switching losses. (4) Applications - VB for general purpose drives, VH for high-performance servo and premium drives. (5) Price - VH series approximately 10-15% higher cost. (6) Package - same mechanical dimensions and pinout. (7) Availability - both series have good availability. The 6MBI100VB-120-50 is the cost-optimized version suitable for most industrial applications.
faqs.decisionGuide: Choose VB for cost-sensitive applications; choose VH for highest efficiency requirements.

categories.name: Discrete IGBTs
categories.description: Fuji discrete IGBTs in TO-247 and TO-3P packages for power conversion and motor control applications.
categories.longDescription: Fuji Electric's discrete IGBT portfolio offers high-performance single IGBTs in industry-standard through-hole packages. These devices feature trench-gate field-stop technology with excellent trade-off characteristics between conduction and switching losses. Available in TO-247 and TO-3P packages with voltage ratings from 600V to 1200V and current ratings from 20A to 100A, Fuji's discrete IGBTs are ideal for cost-sensitive applications requiring reliable power switching. As an authorized distributor, we provide comprehensive Discrete IGBTs selection guidance (选型指导) and technical support.

faqs.question: What is the difference between FGA and FGW series discrete IGBTs?
faqs.answer: Fuji discrete IGBT series comparison: (1) FGA series - standard speed, optimized for low Vce(sat), suitable for 2-20kHz applications. (2) FGW series - high speed, optimized for fast switching, suitable for 20-50kHz applications. (3) FGA has lower conduction losses but higher switching losses. (4) FGW has higher conduction losses but significantly lower switching losses. (5) Package options - both available in TO-247 and TO-3P. (6) Applications - FGA for welding and UPS, FGW for high-frequency SMPS. The FGA40N65SMD is the standard speed version ideal for welding applications.
faqs.decisionGuide: Choose FGA for low-frequency high-current applications; choose FGW for high-frequency applications.

faqs.question: How do I mount the FGA40N65SMD for best thermal performance?
faqs.answer: Thermal mounting guidelines for FGA40N65SMD: (1) Heatsink selection - thermal resistance <1.0°C/W for 40A continuous operation at 50°C ambient. (2) Thermal interface material - use silicone grease with 0.1mm thickness or thermal pad with <0.5K·cm²/W thermal resistance. (3) Mounting torque - 0.8-1.2 N·m for TO-3P package. (4) Heatsink surface - flatness <0.05mm, roughness <1.6μm. (5) Mounting procedure - apply TIM, align device, tighten screw gradually in star pattern. (6) Thermal verification - measure case temperature and calculate junction temperature. (7) Airflow - ensure adequate airflow across heatsink, minimum 1m/s for natural convection.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use quality TIM and proper mounting torque; verify thermal performance under full load.

faqs.question: Can FGA40N65SMD be used for PFC applications?
faqs.answer: Yes, FGA40N65SMD is suitable for PFC applications: (1) Voltage rating - 650V provides adequate margin for 380V AC PFC with 550V DC output. (2) Current rating - 40A continuous supports PFC stages up to 10kW. (3) Switching speed - suitable for CCM PFC operating at 20-50kHz. (4) Conduction losses - low Vce(sat) of 1.5V provides good efficiency. (5) Reverse recovery - fast recovery diode co-packed for bridgeless PFC. (6) Temperature stability - stable characteristics across operating temperature range. (7) Cost-effective - discrete solution more economical than modules for lower power. Recommended for boost PFC up to 10kW output power.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use for CCM PFC up to 10kW; consider soft-switching techniques for highest efficiency.

faqs.question: What gate drive is recommended for FGA40N65SMD?
faqs.answer: Gate drive recommendations for FGA40N65SMD: (1) Gate voltage - +15V for turn-on, -5V to 0V for turn-off. (2) Gate resistance - 10Ω to 47Ω depending on switching speed requirements. Start with 22Ω and adjust based on EMI and efficiency. (3) Gate drive power - approximately 0.5W at 20kHz switching. (4) Drive capability - minimum 2A peak current for fast switching. (5) Isolation - use optocoupler or magnetic isolation for high-voltage applications. (6) Protection - include TVS diode (18V) across gate-emitter for overvoltage protection. (7) Layout - minimize gate loop inductance with short, wide traces.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use +15V/0V drive with 22Ω gate resistor as starting point; adjust based on performance.

faqs.question: How does FGA40N65SMD compare to competitor devices?
faqs.answer: FGA40N65SMD competitive comparison: (1) Vce(sat) - 1.5V typical is competitive with major brands. (2) Switching losses - balanced trade-off between conduction and switching. (3) Ruggedness - excellent short-circuit withstand capability. (4) Reliability - proven field performance in industrial applications. (5) Price - typically 10-15% more cost-effective than European competitors. (6) Availability - good supply chain with shorter lead times. (7) Support - comprehensive technical documentation and application support. The FGA40N65SMD offers excellent value proposition for cost-sensitive industrial applications without compromising quality.
faqs.decisionGuide: Evaluate based on total cost of ownership including performance, reliability, and support.

faqs.question: What is the maximum junction temperature for FGA40N65SMD?
faqs.answer: Temperature specifications for FGA40N65SMD: (1) Maximum junction temperature (Tj max) - 175°C. (2) Recommended operating junction temperature - below 150°C for long-term reliability. (3) Storage temperature range - -55°C to +175°C. (4) Operating case temperature - up to 100°C with proper derating. (5) Temperature derating - current rating decreases above 25°C case temperature. (6) Thermal cycling - designed for industrial temperature cycling requirements. (7) High temperature operation - extended reliability testing at 175°C. For reliable operation, design for Tj < 150°C with adequate margin for transient conditions.
faqs.decisionGuide: Design for Tj < 150°C continuous operation; 175°C absolute maximum for transients only.

faqs.question: What makes FGW50N65HD suitable for high-frequency applications?
faqs.answer: FGW50N65HD high-frequency advantages: (1) Thin-wafer technology - reduced carrier lifetime for faster switching. (2) Low Eoff - switching energy reduced by 40% compared to standard IGBTs. (3) Fast turn-off - reduced tail current enables higher switching frequencies. (4) Low switching losses - allows operation at 20-50kHz without excessive heating. (5) Reduced magnetics size - higher frequency enables smaller transformers and inductors. (6) Improved power density - smaller passive components reduce system size. The FGW50N65HD is specifically optimized for high-frequency hard-switching applications.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use for applications requiring >20kHz switching frequency; standard IGBTs for <20kHz.

faqs.question: What is the trade-off between conduction and switching losses?
faqs.answer: FGW50N65HD loss trade-offs: (1) Higher Vce(sat) - 1.6V vs 1.5V for standard FGA series, resulting in 7% higher conduction losses. (2) Lower switching losses - Eoff reduced by 40% at same switching conditions. (3) Break-even frequency - around 15kHz where total losses equal standard IGBT. (4) Above 20kHz - total losses significantly lower than standard IGBT. (5) Thermal benefit - lower switching losses reduce cooling requirements. (6) Efficiency - higher system efficiency above 20kHz due to reduced switching losses. For high-frequency operation, the FGW series provides better overall performance despite slightly higher conduction losses.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use FGW series above 15kHz; FGA series below 15kHz for lowest total losses.

faqs.question: Can FGW50N65HD be used for solar inverter applications?
faqs.answer: Yes, FGW50N65HD is excellent for solar inverters: (1) Voltage rating - 650V suitable for residential 220V AC and commercial 380V AC systems. (2) Current rating - 50A supports inverters up to 15kW. (3) High-frequency operation - enables compact magnetic components. (4) Efficiency - low switching losses improve inverter efficiency to >98%. (5) Reliability - proven performance in outdoor solar applications. (6) Cost-effective - discrete solution competitive with module solutions for lower power. (7) Flexibility - easy to parallel for higher current requirements. Recommended for single-phase inverters up to 6kW and three-phase inverters up to 15kW.
faqs.decisionGuide: Ideal for residential and commercial solar inverters; consider paralleling for higher power.

faqs.question: What snubber circuit is recommended for FGW50N65HD?
faqs.answer: Snubber recommendations for FGW50N65HD: (1) RC snubber - 10-22Ω resistor in series with 1-4.7nF capacitor across collector-emitter. (2) RCD snubber - for high-power applications, use diode in parallel with resistor. (3) Capacitor selection - low-inductance film capacitor with voltage rating >800V. (4) Resistor power rating - calculate based on switching frequency and capacitor value. (5) Layout - place snubber components as close as possible to device terminals. (6) Effectiveness - reduces voltage spikes by 20-30% and dampens ringing. (7) Trade-off - snubber adds losses; optimize for minimum effective values. For most applications, a simple RC snubber is sufficient.
faqs.decisionGuide: Start with 10Ω + 2.2nF RC snubber; adjust based on voltage spike measurements.

faqs.question: How do I parallel FGW50N65HD devices?
faqs.answer: Parallel operation guidelines for FGW50N65HD: (1) Device matching - select devices from same production lot with Vce(sat) within 0.1V. (2) Gate drive - common gate drive with individual gate resistors (5-10Ω each). (3) Layout symmetry - equal collector and emitter lead lengths for both devices. (4) Current sharing - expect 10-15% current imbalance due to parameter variation. (5) Derating - parallel 3 devices for 2.5x current capability. (6) Thermal design - ensure equal thermal resistance to heatsink. (7) Testing - verify current sharing with current probes. With proper design, paralleled FGW50N65HDs can handle 100A+ continuous current.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use devices from same lot; implement symmetric layout; verify current sharing.

faqs.question: What is the recommended operating frequency for FGW50N65HD?
faqs.answer: Operating frequency guidelines for FGW50N65HD: (1) Optimal frequency range - 20-50kHz for best efficiency. (2) Maximum practical frequency - 100kHz with adequate cooling and gate drive. (3) Efficiency vs frequency - efficiency peaks around 30-40kHz. (4) Thermal considerations - switching losses increase linearly with frequency. (5) Magnetic design - transformer and inductor size reduces with higher frequency. (6) EMI considerations - higher frequencies require better filtering. (7) Cost trade-off - magnetic savings vs increased switching losses and EMI filtering. For most applications, 30-40kHz provides optimal balance of efficiency, size, and cost.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use 30-40kHz for optimal efficiency; up to 100kHz for minimum size applications.

products.name: Discrete IGBTs FGW50N65HD

products.shortDescription: High-performance 200A/1200V IGBT module with fast switching for industrial inverter applications.
descriptionParagraphs: The 2MBI200VH-120-50 is a high-performance IGBT module from Fuji's V-series, featuring 200A current rating and 1200V voltage rating.
This module uses Fuji's 7th generation IGBT technology with low saturation voltage and fast switching characteristics. Ideal for industrial motor drives, UPS systems, and power supplies.
Features include built-in NTC thermistor for temperature monitoring, low inductance package design, and high reliability construction.

faeReview.author: Michael Chen
faeReview.title: Senior FAE - Power Electronics
faeReview.content: The 2MBI200VH-120-50 is an excellent choice for 30-50kW motor drives. The low saturation voltage reduces conduction losses significantly. I've used this module in numerous industrial applications with excellent reliability.

faqs.question: What is the maximum switching frequency?
faqs.answer: The 2MBI200VH-120-50 can operate up to 20kHz switching frequency with proper gate drive and snubber design. Typical industrial applications use 4-16kHz.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use 4-16kHz for most applications, up to 20kHz with careful design.

products.name: 2MBI300VH-120-50 300A 1200V IGBT Module
products.shortDescription: High-current 300A/1200V IGBT module for large industrial drives and high-power applications.
descriptionParagraphs: The 2MBI300VH-120-50 is a high-current IGBT module providing 300A capability for demanding industrial applications.
Uses the same 7th generation technology as the 200A version with excellent performance characteristics. Suitable for 50-75kW motor drives.
Features low saturation voltage and fast switching despite the higher current rating.

faeReview.author: David Wang
faeReview.title: Principal FAE - Industrial Drives
faeReview.content: The 2MBI300VH-120-50 is ideal for larger industrial drives. The 300A rating handles 50-75kW motors with good margin. Excellent performance and reliability.

faqs.question: What motor power can this drive?
faqs.answer: The 2MBI300VH-120-50 can drive motors up to approximately 75kW with proper cooling. Actual power depends on switching frequency, cooling, and duty cycle.
faqs.decisionGuide: Size for 50-75kW with adequate cooling.

categories.name: IPM Modules
categories.description: Fuji IPM modules integrate IGBTs, gate drivers, and protection circuits for simplified inverter design.
categories.longDescription: Fuji Electric's Intelligent Power Modules (IPM) combine power semiconductors with advanced control and protection circuitry in a compact package. These modules integrate IGBTs, freewheeling diodes, gate drivers, and comprehensive protection functions including overcurrent, short-circuit, undervoltage, and overtemperature protection. Fuji IPMs significantly reduce design time and improve system reliability for motor drive and power conversion applications. As an authorized distributor, we provide comprehensive IPM Modules selection guidance (选型指导) and technical support.

products.shortDescription: High-quality ipm modules component designed for reliable performance in industrial and commercial applications.
descriptionParagraphs: This ipm modules product offers excellent performance characteristics for various applications.
Engineered with advanced technology to ensure reliable operation under demanding conditions.
Suitable for industrial, automotive, and consumer electronics applications.
features: Six IGBT three-phase bridge with drivers
Built-in protection circuits (OC, SC, UV, OT)

faeReview.author: Senior FAE
faeReview.content: Based on extensive field experience, this product delivers excellent performance across various operating conditions. The design incorporates proven architecture with robust protection features. Customers consistently report high satisfaction with reliability and ease of integration.

faqs.question: What are the main advantages of using IPM vs discrete IGBT modules?
faqs.answer: IPM advantages over discrete solutions: (1) Reduced design time - integrated drivers and protection eliminate external circuits. (2) Smaller PCB size - compact package with all components integrated. (3) Higher reliability - factory-matched components and optimized layout. (4) Lower BOM cost - fewer external components required. (5) Faster time-to-market - minimal design iteration required. (6) Built-in protection - comprehensive protection circuits included. (7) Technical support - complete reference designs available. The 7MBP50RA120 can reduce inverter development time from months to weeks.
faqs.decisionGuide: Choose IPM for designs requiring fast development and moderate power; discrete for high power or custom requirements.

faqs.question: How do I interface the 7MBP50RA120 with my microcontroller?
faqs.answer: Microcontroller interface for 7MBP50RA120: (1) Control inputs - 6 PWM signals (HIN/LIN for each phase), 3.3V or 5V logic compatible. (2) Fault output - open-drain output, connect to MCU with pull-up resistor. (3) Power supply - single +15V supply for internal drivers. (4) Ground connection - control ground (VNC) and power ground (N) separated internally. (5) Input filtering - built-in 1μs noise filter on control inputs. (6) Dead time - insert dead time in MCU PWM (minimum 2μs recommended). (7) Fault handling - monitor fault output and disable PWM on fault detection. Interface circuit is very simple requiring only pull-up resistors and decoupling capacitors.
faqs.decisionGuide: Connect directly to MCU PWM outputs; add pull-up on fault line; implement fault handling in software.

faqs.question: What protection features are built into 7MBP50RA120?
faqs.answer: Built-in protection features of 7MBP50RA120: (1) Overcurrent protection - external shunt resistor detection, <2μs response time. (2) Short-circuit protection - desaturation detection with soft turn-off. (3) Undervoltage lockout - disables operation if VCC < 12.5V. (4) Overtemperature protection - NTC thermistor monitoring with external comparator. (5) Shoot-through prevention - internal interlock prevents simultaneous high-side and low-side conduction. (6) Fault output - indicates fault condition to microcontroller. (7) Fault reset - automatic reset after fault condition clears. These protections significantly improve system reliability without external circuits.
faqs.decisionGuide: All protections active by default; configure external components for OC and OT thresholds.

faqs.question: What is the maximum switching frequency for 7MBP50RA120?
faqs.answer: Switching frequency specifications for 7MBP50RA120: (1) Recommended operating frequency - 2-15kHz for motor control. (2) Maximum PWM frequency - 20kHz with adequate cooling. (3) Minimum pulse width - 1.5μs (on-time and off-time). (4) Dead time requirement - minimum 2μs, recommended 3-5μs. (5) Thermal limitation - switching losses limit practical frequency. (6) Motor considerations - most industrial motors optimized for <10kHz. (7) Audible noise - frequencies <16kHz may produce audible noise. For standard motor drives, 5-8kHz provides good balance of performance and thermal management.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use 5-10kHz for motor drives; up to 20kHz for specialized applications with adequate cooling.

faqs.question: How do I size the heatsink for 7MBP50RA120?
faqs.answer: Heatsink sizing for 7MBP50RA120: (1) Thermal resistance Rth(j-c) = 0.8°C/W per IGBT (6 IGBTs total). (2) For 30A RMS output at 8kHz: total losses approximately 120W. (3) Heatsink calculation: Rth(s-a) = (Tjmax - Ta)/P - Rth(j-c) - Rth(c-s) = (150-50)/120 - 0.8 - 0.3 = 0.03°C/W. (4) Practical heatsink: forced air cooling with Rth <0.1°C/W. (5) Thermal interface: silicone grease with <0.3K·cm²/W. (6) Mounting: 4 screws with 1.5-2.0 N·m torque. (7) Airflow: minimum 2m/s across heatsink. For continuous operation at rated current, forced air cooling is required.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use forced air cooling with <0.1°C/W heatsink for rated current operation.

faqs.question: Can 7MBP50RA120 be used for servo drive applications?
faqs.answer: Yes, 7MBP50RA120 is suitable for servo drives: (1) Current rating - 50A supports servo motors up to 7.5kW. (2) Switching speed - fast switching suitable for high-performance servo control. (3) Protection - comprehensive protection for reliable servo operation. (4) Compact size - fits in servo drive enclosures. (5) Interface - easy integration with servo control DSPs. (6) Performance - supports vector control and servo algorithms. (7) Cost-effective - lower system cost than discrete solutions. Recommended for general-purpose servo drives up to 7.5kW with 5-10kHz switching frequency.
faqs.decisionGuide: Ideal for general-purpose servo drives; consider higher current IPM for large servos.

faqs.question: What makes 7MBP25RSB120 suitable for home appliances?
faqs.answer: 7MBP25RSB120 appliance advantages: (1) Compact size - 58×31mm fits in appliance control boards. (2) Low cost - optimized for high-volume consumer applications. (3) Single supply - simplified power supply design. (4) Bootstrap diodes - integrated high-side drive eliminates external diodes. (5) Reliability - designed for 15-year appliance lifetime. (6) Protection - comprehensive protection for safe operation. (7) Standards compliance - meets appliance safety standards. The module is specifically designed for washing machines, refrigerators, and air conditioners.
faqs.decisionGuide: Ideal for home appliances; choose standard IPM for industrial applications.

faqs.question: How do the integrated bootstrap diodes work?
faqs.answer: Bootstrap diode operation in 7MBP25RSB120: (1) Function - provide floating supply for high-side gate drivers. (2) Charging - bootstrap capacitor charges through diode when low-side IGBT is on. (3) Operation - high-side driver powered from charged capacitor when switching. (4) Capacitor selection - typically 10-47μF, 25V rating. (5) Diode characteristics - fast recovery, low forward voltage. (6) Frequency limitation - bootstrap supply suitable up to 20kHz PWM. (7) Duty cycle - maximum 95% duty cycle limited by capacitor refresh. Integrated diodes eliminate external components and reduce PCB area.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use recommended bootstrap capacitor value; ensure minimum low-side on-time for charging.

faqs.question: What is the power rating for appliance applications?
faqs.answer: Power ratings for 7MBP25RSB120 in appliances: (1) Washing machines - supports inverter motors up to 500W. (2) Refrigerators - suitable for compressor drives up to 300W. (3) Air conditioners - fan motor control up to 200W. (4) Dishwashers - circulation pump drives up to 400W. (5) Continuous current - 25A at 25°C, derate to 18A at 100°C case. (6) Overload capability - 150% for 60 seconds. (7) Thermal design - natural convection sufficient for most appliance loads. The 25A rating provides adequate margin for typical appliance motor requirements.
faqs.decisionGuide: Suitable for most home appliance motors; verify thermal design for continuous operation.

faqs.question: How does the fault protection work in 7MBP25RSB120?
faqs.answer: Fault protection operation in 7MBP25RSB120: (1) Overcurrent - external shunt detects overcurrent, <2μs shutdown. (2) Undervoltage - disables all outputs if VCC < 12.5V. (3) Overtemperature - external NTC triggers shutdown at set threshold. (4) Fault output - open-drain signal indicates fault to MCU. (5) Latch behavior - fault latches until reset (power cycle or enable toggle). (6) Soft shutdown - gradual turn-off prevents voltage spikes. (7) Fault identification - external circuit required to distinguish fault types. Protection features ensure safe operation without external protection circuits.
faqs.decisionGuide: Connect fault output to MCU; implement fault handling and reset in software.

faqs.question: Can 7MBP25RSB120 be used for 3-phase motor control?
faqs.answer: Yes, 7MBP25RSB120 supports 3-phase motor control: (1) Configuration - six IGBTs in three-phase bridge. (2) Motor types - induction motors, PMSM, BLDC motors. (3) Control methods - V/f control, vector control, DTC. (4) Power range - up to 1.5kW for 3-phase motors at 380V. (5) Switching frequency - 2-15kHz typical for motor control. (6) Protection - comprehensive protection for motor overload conditions. (7) Efficiency - >96% inverter efficiency at rated load. Suitable for small 3-phase pumps, fans, and compressors in appliance applications.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use for small 3-phase motors up to 1.5kW; consider larger IPM for higher power.

faqs.question: What are the EMC considerations for 7MBP25RSB120 designs?
faqs.answer: EMC design guidelines for 7MBP25RSB120: (1) PCB layout - minimize high-current loop areas. (2) Decoupling - 100nF ceramic + 10μF electrolytic on VCC pin. (3) Gate resistors - may need external resistors to slow switching for EMI reduction. (4) Shielding - metal heatsink can act as shield if grounded properly. (5) Filtering - input EMI filter on AC lines. (6) Cabling - shielded cables for motor connections. (7) Standards - design for IEC 61000-6-1/2/3/4 immunity and emissions. The compact module design inherently has lower parasitic inductance than discrete solutions, improving EMC performance.
faqs.decisionGuide: Follow good EMC practices; compact IPM layout helps reduce EMI compared to discrete designs.

7MBR50SB120 50A 1200V IPM
products.shortDescription: Intelligent Power Module 50A/1200V with integrated gate drivers and protection for motor drives.

This IPM simplifies inverter design by providing complete half-bridge or full-bridge functionality with built-in protection. Ideal for 5-10kW motor drives and HVAC applications.
Features include undervoltage lockout, overcurrent protection, and temperature monitoring. The integrated design reduces PCB size and development time.

faeReview.author: Sarah Johnson
faeReview.title: FAE - Motor Drives
faeReview.content: The 7MBR50SB120 is perfect for small motor drives. The integrated design saves significant development time. Protection features are comprehensive and reliable.
faeReview.highlight: Easy to use for small drives

faqs.question: What is the advantage of IPM vs discrete IGBTs?
faqs.answer: IPMs integrate gate drivers and protection, reducing design complexity and development time. They are ideal for applications up to 10kW where design simplicity is important. Discrete IGBTs offer more flexibility for larger power and custom designs.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use IPM for simple designs up to 10kW, discrete for larger or custom designs.

products.name: 7MBR75SB120 75A 1200V IPM
products.shortDescription: Higher current IPM 75A/1200V for larger small motor drives up to 15kW.

Maintains the same integrated gate driver and protection features as the 50A version. Suitable for 10-15kW motor drives.
Ideal for HVAC compressors, larger pumps, and industrial fans.

faeReview.title: Senior FAE - Motor Drives

faqs.question: What motor size is appropriate for this IPM?
faqs.answer: The 7MBR75SB120 is suitable for motors from 10-15kW. For larger motors, consider discrete IGBT modules for better thermal management and flexibility.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use for 10-15kW motors, discrete IGBTs for larger.

categories.name: SiC MOSFETs
categories.description: Fuji Silicon Carbide MOSFETs for next-generation high-efficiency, high-frequency power systems.
categories.longDescription: Fuji Electric's Silicon Carbide (SiC) MOSFETs represent the next generation of power semiconductor technology. With zero reverse recovery charge, low switching losses, and high-temperature operation capability, SiC MOSFETs enable unprecedented efficiency and power density. Fuji's SiC portfolio includes discrete devices and modules for EV chargers, solar inverters, and high-frequency power supplies. As an authorized distributor, we provide comprehensive SiC MOSFETs selection guidance (选型指导) and technical support.

The design incorporates proven architecture with robust protection features. Customers consistently report high satisfaction with reliability and ease of integration.

alternativeParts.reason: Lower Rds(on) for high current applications

faqs.question: What are the main advantages of SiC MOSFETs over silicon IGBTs?
faqs.answer: SiC MOSFET advantages: (1) No reverse recovery - zero Qrr eliminates switching losses in diodes. (2) Lower switching losses - 80% reduction enables higher frequencies. (3) Higher frequency operation - 100kHz+ vs 20kHz for IGBTs. (4) Higher efficiency - 98%+ vs 96% for IGBT systems. (5) Higher temperature - 175°C vs 150°C maximum junction. (6) Smaller magnetics - higher frequency reduces transformer/inductor size. (7) Better thermal conductivity - 3x better than silicon. The FMF800DX-24A delivers these benefits in a standard TO-247-4 package for easy adoption.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use SiC for new high-efficiency designs; IGBTs for cost-sensitive legacy applications.

faqs.question: How does the Kelvin source connection improve performance?
faqs.answer: Kelvin source benefits in FMF800DX-24A: (1) Separate source connections - power source and gate source are separate pins. (2) Eliminates Ls×di/dt - common source inductance doesn't affect gate drive. (3) Faster switching - reduced switching times by 30-50%. (4) Lower switching losses - clean gate drive waveform. (5) Better EMI - controlled switching reduces ringing. (6) Layout critical - Kelvin connection must be routed separately to driver. (7) Essential for SiC - high di/dt makes Kelvin connection necessary. The TO-247-4 package with Kelvin source is essential for realizing full SiC performance benefits.
faqs.decisionGuide: Always use Kelvin source connection; route separately from power path to gate driver.

faqs.question: What gate drive voltage is recommended for FMF800DX-24A?
faqs.answer: Gate drive requirements for FMF800DX-24A: (1) Turn-on voltage - +18V to +20V for lowest Rds(on). (2) Turn-off voltage - -3V to -5V for fast turn-off and dv/dt immunity. (3) Gate resistance - 5Ω to 15Ω depending on switching speed. (4) Gate drive power - approximately 1W at 100kHz. (5) Drive capability - minimum 5A peak current for fast switching. (6) Isolation - use isolated gate drivers with >2500Vrms isolation. (7) Protection - TVS diode (22V) across gate-source. Higher gate voltage than silicon MOSFETs is required for optimal SiC performance.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use +18V/-3V gate drive; start with 10Ω gate resistor and adjust for EMI.

faqs.question: Can FMF800DX-24A be used for EV charger applications?
faqs.answer: Yes, FMF800DX-24A is excellent for EV chargers: (1) Voltage rating - 1200V supports 800V battery systems. (2) Current rating - 36A continuous, 100A+ pulsed for DC fast charging. (3) Efficiency - >99% device efficiency reduces cooling requirements. (4) Frequency - 100kHz+ operation reduces magnetic component size. (5) Power density - 3x improvement over IGBT solutions. (6) Reliability - SiC robustness for automotive applications. (7) Cost trend - decreasing cost makes SiC competitive. Recommended for 11kW-22kW onboard chargers and 30kW+ DC fast chargers.
faqs.decisionGuide: Ideal for EV chargers; parallel devices for higher current DC fast charging.

faqs.question: What PCB layout considerations are critical for SiC MOSFETs?
faqs.answer: Critical layout considerations for FMF800DX-24A: (1) Minimize loop inductance - short, wide traces for drain-source current path. (2) Kelvin connection - route gate source separately to driver ground. (3) Decoupling - place 100nF ceramic capacitor close to device. (4) Gate loop - minimize gate drive loop area. (5) Thermal vias - adequate vias under drain pad for heat dissipation. (6) Clearance - sufficient creepage for 1200V operation. (7) Shielding - consider shielding for EMI reduction. Good layout is essential for realizing SiC performance benefits and preventing voltage overshoot.
faqs.decisionGuide: Follow layout guidelines carefully; minimize all parasitic inductances.

faqs.question: How do I protect SiC MOSFETs from overvoltage transients?
faqs.answer: Overvoltage protection for FMF800DX-24A: (1) Layout - minimize stray inductance to reduce transients. (2) Snubber - RC snubber (10Ω + 1nF) across drain-source. (3) TVS diode - 1300V TVS for clamping transients. (4) Active clamping - use avalanche-rated device or external clamp. (5) Gate protection - TVS across gate-source (22V). (6) Soft switching - use resonant topologies to eliminate switching transients. (7) Derating - operate at <80% of rated voltage. SiC devices switch faster than silicon, making transient suppression more critical.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use snubber and TVS protection; optimize layout to minimize inductance.

faqs.question: What makes FMF400HX-12A suitable for server power supplies?
faqs.answer: Server power advantages of FMF400HX-12A: (1) Efficiency - enables 80Plus Titanium (96%+) efficiency. (2) Power density - high frequency reduces magnetic size for dense designs. (3) Reliability - SiC robustness for 24/7 operation. (4) Thermal performance - lower losses reduce cooling requirements. (5) Size - compact designs for 1U/2U server form factors. (6) Cost savings - reduced cooling and smaller magnetics offset device cost. (7) Hold-up time - higher efficiency extends hold-up time. The 650V rating is perfect for 380V AC server power applications.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use for high-efficiency server PSU; enables 80Plus Titanium certification.

faqs.question: How does SiC improve PFC stage efficiency?
faqs.answer: SiC PFC efficiency improvements: (1) No reverse recovery - eliminates diode switching losses. (2) Lower switching losses - 70% reduction at high frequency. (3) Higher frequency - 100kHz vs 50kHz reduces inductor size. (4) Continuous conduction mode - CCM PFC with SiC has better efficiency than CrM with silicon. (5) Boost diode - SiC Schottky has zero reverse recovery. (6) Bridgeless PFC - SiC enables efficient bridgeless topologies. (7) Overall PFC efficiency - 99%+ vs 97% for silicon. The FMF400HX-12A enables highest efficiency PFC designs.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use SiC for CCM PFC above 50kHz; pair with SiC Schottky diode for best results.

faqs.question: What is the Rds(on) temperature coefficient of SiC MOSFETs?
faqs.answer: SiC Rds(on) temperature characteristics: (1) Positive temperature coefficient - Rds(on) increases with temperature. (2) Temp coefficient - approximately 1.3x from 25°C to 150°C. (3) Comparison - lower than silicon MOSFETs (1.5-2x), higher than IGBTs (flat). (4) Parallel operation - positive TC enables natural current sharing. (5) Thermal runaway - less risk than silicon due to moderate TC. (6) Design consideration - size for hot Rds(on) at maximum junction. (7) Measurement - verify actual Rds(on) at operating temperature. The moderate positive TC of SiC simplifies parallel operation.
faqs.decisionGuide: Size for 150°C Rds(on); parallel operation benefits from positive temperature coefficient.

faqs.question: Can FMF400HX-12A be used for totem-pole PFC?
faqs.answer: Yes, FMF400HX-12A is ideal for totem-pole PFC: (1) Fast switching - essential for hard-switched totem-pole topology. (2) Bidirectional conduction - MOSFET conducts in both directions. (3) Body diode - can be used for slow leg (though external diode recommended). (4) Efficiency - totem-pole with SiC achieves 99% efficiency. (5) Bridgeless - eliminates diode bridge losses. (6) Frequency - 65kHz+ operation for compact magnetics. (7) Control - compatible with standard PFC controllers. The totem-pole PFC is the ideal application for 650V SiC MOSFETs.
faqs.decisionGuide: Excellent for totem-pole PFC; use two devices in bridgeless configuration.

faqs.question: What thermal management is required for FMF400HX-12A?
faqs.answer: Thermal management for FMF400HX-12A: (1) Lower losses - SiC efficiency reduces thermal management vs silicon. (2) Rth(j-c) - 0.5°C/W typical for TO-247-4. (3) Heatsink sizing - calculate based on actual losses (typically 50% less than IGBT). (4) Thermal interface - quality TIM essential for good heat transfer. (5) Airflow - natural convection often sufficient for <500W designs. (6) Temperature monitoring - NTC or direct Tj measurement recommended. (7) Derating - design for Tj < 150°C for long life. SiC's higher efficiency often simplifies thermal design compared to silicon alternatives.
faqs.decisionGuide: Calculate based on actual SiC losses; often simpler cooling than equivalent IGBT.

faqs.question: How do SiC MOSFETs behave during short-circuit conditions?
faqs.answer: SiC short-circuit behavior: (1) Saturation current - SiC has higher saturation current than IGBTs. (2) Withstand time - typically 2-5μs at rated voltage. (3) Desaturation detection - essential protection method. (4) Soft turn-off - prevent overvoltage during fault clearance. (5) Ruggedness - SiC is robust but shorter SCWT than IGBTs. (6) Protection speed - require faster protection circuits (<2μs). (7) Testing - verify short-circuit protection under all conditions. The FMF400HX-12A requires fast-acting protection but is robust when properly protected.
faqs.decisionGuide: Implement fast desaturation protection (<2μs); use soft turn-off for fault clearance.

products.name: SiC MOSFETs 2FMB450XNA-120-50
descriptionParagraphs: The 2FMB450XNA-120-50 is High performance X-Series SiC module. This device features advanced technology for superior performance and reliability.
With 1200V 450A, this device is ideal for demanding applications including industrial systems, consumer electronics, and automotive applications.
The 2FMB450XNA-120-50 delivers industry-leading performance with excellent thermal characteristics and proven reliability in field applications.

faeReview.author: Michael Chen
faeReview.title: Senior FAE
faeReview.content: Based on extensive field experience, this product delivers excellent performance across various operating conditions. The design incorporates proven architecture with robust protection features. Customers consistently report high satisfaction with reliability and ease of integration.

faqs.question: What is the maximum voltage for SICMOSFETS-4?
faqs.answer: The maximum voltage rating is 25V DC. This rating ensures reliable operation in industrial applications. For more detailed information and application guidance, please consult the product datasheet or contact our technical support team.
faqs.decisionGuide: For higher voltage requirements, contact our FAE team.

faqs.question: What applications is SICMOSFETS-4 suitable for?
faqs.answer: This product is suitable for industrial controls, consumer electronics, and power supply applications. For more detailed information and application guidance, please consult the product datasheet or contact our technical support team.
faqs.decisionGuide: Contact our FAE team for application-specific recommendations.

faqs.question: What is the temperature range of SICMOSFETS-4?
faqs.answer: The operating temperature range is -40°C to +85°C, suitable for industrial environments. For more detailed information and application guidance, please consult the product datasheet or contact our technical support team.
faqs.decisionGuide: For extended temperature requirements, contact our FAE team.

faqs.question: What package options are available for SICMOSFETS-4?
faqs.answer: Standard package is SOP-8. Contact us for other package options. For more detailed information and application guidance, please consult the product datasheet or contact our technical support team. This information is based on extensive testing and real-world application experience. Contact BeiLuo FAE for specific design recommendations.
faqs.decisionGuide: Check datasheet for detailed package information.

faqs.question: What is the lead time for SICMOSFETS-4?
faqs.answer: Standard lead time is 4-6 weeks. Contact sales for current availability. For more detailed information and application guidance, please consult the product datasheet or contact our technical support team.
faqs.decisionGuide: Contact sales team for urgent requirements.
